codeReview.summary: Commit 2ca10d9 fixes the four blockers it explicitly targeted: BiometricVault.initialize() now derives from locally-held entropy and passes `secretHex` into native provisioning (`src/lib/enbox/biometric-vault.ts:597`) so the only remaining `getSecret()` call is in unlock (`src/lib/enbox/biometric-vault.ts:698`); agent-store now calls `agent.initialize({})` / `agent.start({})` with no password field (`src/lib/enbox/agent-store.ts:153`, `src/lib/enbox/agent-store.ts:158`, `src/lib/enbox/agent-store.ts:212`); reset now clears both persisted vault flags and wipes ENBOX_AGENT LevelDB via `destroyAgentLevelDatabases()` (`src/lib/enbox/agent-store.ts:330`, `src/lib/enbox/agent-store.ts:338`, `src/lib/enbox/agent-store.ts:353`, `src/lib/enbox/rn-level.ts:332-376`). The milestone still has one previously synthesized blocking issue unresolved, though: the auto-lock hook remains on the old timer-based lock-only implementation.

issues.description: The prior auto-lock blocker remains genuinely unfixed. `useAutoLock` still depends on `AUTO_LOCK_TIMEOUT_MS` (`src/hooks/use-auto-lock.ts:4,23,31`) and only calls `session.lock()` on `background` / `inactive`; it never calls `useAgentStore.getState().teardown()`, and there is still no dedicated `use-auto-lock` hook test file under `src/hooks/`. `validation-contract.md:1700-1744` still requires teardown-on-background plus a fresh biometric prompt after auto-lock (VAL-VAULT-020/021), so the milestone cannot yet pass scrutiny even though the four blockers addressed by this fix commit are resolved. The earlier `auto-lock-vault-primitives` feature description was narrower than that contract, but the code gap itself is real rather than misattributed.

summary: Reviewed the fix transcript skeleton, handoff, commit diff, prior reviews, and the original failed diffs. The rerun successfully fixes the four scoped biometric-vault / agent-store reset blockers, but scrutiny still fails for the milestone because the previously synthesized auto-lock blocker was not addressed and `useAutoLock` still lacks teardown-on-background behavior and coverage.
